text:
Let me start by discovering the data I need.

**Step 1: Find the unresolved Sentry issue with the most users_affected.**

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT id, title, project, users_affected, status FROM sentry_issues WHERE status = 'unresolved' ORDER BY users_affected DESC LIMIT 1;"}

text:
Great! The top unresolved Sentry issue is **SENTRY-3028** ("5xx: Image uploads time out over 5MB") affecting service **api-gateway** with 863 users.

**Step 2: Find triggered PagerDuty incidents for 'api-gateway' and acknowledge them.**

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT id, title, status, service FROM pagerduty_incidents WHERE service = 'api-gateway' AND status = 'triggered';"}

text: Let me acknowledge that incident by updating its status.

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"UPDATE pagerduty_incidents SET status = 'acknowledged' WHERE id = 'PD-424' AND service = 'api-gateway' AND status = 'triggered' RETURNING id, status;"}

message: glove-scratchpad: RETURNING on UPDATE is not supported — run the update, then SELECT the rows (your write is reflected this session: read-your-writes).

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"UPDATE pagerduty_incidents SET status = 'acknowledged' WHERE id = 'PD-424' AND service = 'api-gateway' AND status = 'triggered';"}

text: Let me verify it was acknowledged and check for any other triggered incidents for that service.

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT id, status FROM pagerduty_incidents WHERE service = 'api-gateway';"}

text: Now let me find the 'incidents' Slack channel and create the Linear issue.

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT id, name FROM slack_channels WHERE name = 'incidents';"}

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"INSERT INTO linear_issues (id, title, state, team, project) VALUES ('LIN-1', 'Postmortem: 5xx: Image uploads time out over 5MB', 'todo', 'Engineering', 'api-gateway');"}

text: Now post a message to the 'incidents' Slack channel:

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"INSERT INTO slack_messages (channel, user, text) VALUES ('C03', 'incident-commander', '🚨 Incident affecting service: **api-gateway** — Sentry issue SENTRY-3028 (5xx: Image uploads time out over 5MB) with 863 users affected. PagerDuty incident PD-424 acknowledged.');"}

text: Now send the email with the required details:
